questions.question: Is the new file at the correct path and do all 6 invariant tests (C0, C1, C2, C3, A13, A15) plus 2 observability tests exist?
questions.expected: File exists; grep returns 8 (6 invariants + 2 observability)
questions.actual_at_time_of_writing: File exists at src/store/multi-client.property.test.ts; 8 it() blocks: [AC-X-OBSERVABILITY-1] x2, [C0], [C1], [C2], [C3], [A13], [A15]

questions.question: Does FakeBoard correctly track deliveredTo (never re-delivers), and is quiesce() total (every bus slot reaches every client)?
questions.how_to_check: npx vitest run src/store/multi-client.property.test.ts && grep -A5 'quiesce' src/store/multi-client.property.test.ts | head -20
questions.expected: All tests pass; quiesce loops over all bus slots × all clients and calls deliver(), which skips if deliveredTo already contains target
questions.notes: A13 test explicitly clears deliveredTo to force re-delivery and verifies idempotence. FakeBoard.deliver() guards on deliveredTo.has(targetIdx).

questions.question: Is the total test count >= 69 + 6 = 75? (Baseline was 69, S4 adds 8 tests.)
Do all test titles start with the invariant ID in brackets (AC-X-NAMING-2), and does each body begin with a comment line matching // {ID}: {description} (AC-X-NAMING-1)?
questions.expected: All 8 it() titles start with [ID], e.g. '[C0] settled-state equality (task subset)'; first line of each body is a comment with the invariant ID

questions.question: Does FAST_CHECK_NUM_RUNS=10000 complete in <60 seconds and pass?
questions.how_to_check: time FAST_CHECK_NUM_RUNS=10000 npx vitest run src/store/multi-client.property.test.ts

questions.question: Is no production code changed? (AC-X-NO-PROD-CHANGE-1)
questions.expected: No output — only multi-client.property.test.ts is new; all other src/ files unchanged

questions.question: Does the schedule distribution fc.statistics report show non-degenerate schedules (at least some with >1 event and mixed event types)?
questions.how_to_check: FAST_CHECK_NUM_RUNS=200 npx vitest run src/store/multi-client.property.test.ts --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | grep -A 10 'schedule distribution'
questions.expected: Statistics output shows a mix of clients=2..5, events=1..20, dense-pre-delivery and sparse-pre-delivery; dominant event type distribution shows all 4 types (task.created, task.status_changed, task.assigned, task.deleted)

questions.question: Is the single-writer constraint (all events dispatched by client 0) correctly documented and is the multi-client limitation (concurrent multi-client dispatch = D1 divergence) noted in the file?
questions.expected: Multiple comments explaining single-writer model, MLS linearization rationale, and the concurrent dispatch D1 divergence exclusion
